metadata.title: A Rate-Distortion View of Uncertainty Quantification
metadata.description: This poster presents Distance-Aware Bottleneck (DAB), a method for efficient uncertainty quantification that views the problem through a rate-distortion lens, aiming for reliable, lightweight, and sampling-free models.

sections.title: Problem: Efficient Uncertainty Quantification
content: Goal: reliable & lightweight models that "know what they know."

sections.title: Our Method: Distance-Aware Bottleneck (DAB)
content: 1> Treat the training dataset as a source generating training examples.
2> Map each datapoint to a $distribution$ through a stochastic encoder.

3> The codebook contains the centroids of the encoders in terms of a $statistical\ distance$. Uncertainty is quantified by the expected distance from the codebook: $uncertainty(x_{test}) = \mathbb{E}[D(p(z \mid x_{test}; \theta), q_k(z; \phi))]$

sections.title: Codebook Visualization
content: Number of CIFAR-10 test datapoints assigned to each centroid during training. Each centroid progressively attracts datapoints of the same class.

sections.title: DAB's performance on ImageNet 1-K
content: DAB outperforms ensembles at predicting misclassifications!
DAB can better distinguish out-of-distribution images.

sections.title: References
content: [1] Alemi, A. A., Fischer, I., Dillon, J. V., and Murphy, K. Deep variational information bottleneck. In International Conference on Learning Representations, 2017.
[2] Alemi, A. A., Fischer, I., and Dillon, J. V. Uncertainty in the variational information bottleneck. arXiv preprint arXiv:1807.00906, 2018

[3] Liu, J., Lin, Z., Padhy, S., Tran, D., Bedrax Weiss, T., and Lakshminarayanan, B. Simple and principled uncertainty estimation with deterministic deep learning via distance awareness. Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 2020.
[4] Banerjee, A., Merugu, S., Dhillon, I. S., Ghosh, J., and Lafferty, J. Clustering with Bregman divergences. Journal of Machine Learning Research, 2005.
[5] Van Amersfoort, J., Smith, L., Teh, Y. W., and Gal, Y. Uncertainty estimation using a single deep deterministic neural network. In International Conference on Learning Representations, 2020.
